articles.title: Luka Doncic on a “Slow Path” Back from Injury
articles.original_title: Lakers Injury Update: Luka Doncic On ‘Slow Path’ Towards Return - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: The Lakers reported that Dallas Mavericks star Luka Doncic is progressing cautiously after his recent injury, emphasizing a gradual rehabilitation timeline rather than a rushed return. Team medical staff expect his comeback to be measured to ensure full recovery before the season resumes.
key_points: Doncic’s rehab is being handled conservatively, with no set return date.
Lakers’ medical team is coordinating with the Mavericks to monitor his progress.
The focus is on long‑term health rather than a quick return for the playoffs.

articles.title: Jalen Williams’ Hamstring Strain Clouds Thunder’s Playoff Outlook
articles.original_title: Jalen Williams status uncertain for Thunder with hamstring strain
articles.summary: Thunder forward Jalen Williams suffered a hamstring strain in Game 2 of the first‑round series against the Phoenix Suns, leaving his availability for the remainder of the series uncertain. The injury mirrors recent hamstring issues that have sidelined key players in the league, including the Lakers.
key_points: Williams exited Game 2 with a hamstring strain, status for upcoming games unknown.
The injury adds to the Thunder’s depth concerns in a tightly contested series.
Similar hamstring problems have recently affected other playoff teams, such as the Lakers.

articles.title: Alperen Şengün Addresses Trade Rumors After Rockets’ Loss to Lakers
articles.original_title: Alperen Sengun Reacts to NBA Trade Rumors After Rockets' Loss to Lakers in NBA Playoffs - Bleacher Report
articles.summary: Following Houston’s defeat to the Los Angeles Lakers in the playoffs, Rockets forward Alpar Şengün commented on circulating trade speculation, stating he is focused on improving his game and the team’s performance. He downplayed rumors and emphasized staying committed to the Rockets’ playoff run.
key_points: Şengün dismissed trade chatter, keeping his focus on the postseason.
He highlighted the need for personal and team improvement after the loss.
The comments came amid heightened speculation about roster moves for both teams.

articles.title: JJ Redick Praises Ayton and Hayes for Defensive Effort in Game 6
articles.original_title: JJ Redick praised Deandre Ayton, Jaxson Hayes for ‘phenomenal’ defense in Game 6
articles.summary: Head coach JJ Redick lauded center Deandre Ayton and forward Jaxson Hayes for their “phenomenal” defensive performance in Game 6 against the Houston Rockets. Their effort helped limit Rockets star Alperen Şengün, who averaged a double‑double in the series, contributing to the Lakers’ win.
key_points: Ayton and Hayes received credit for disrupting Şengün’s production.
Redick highlighted their defensive intensity as a key factor in the victory.
The Lakers shifted from double‑team schemes to rely more on individual defense.

articles.title: Deandre Ayton Takes Redick’s Challenge to Guard Alperen Şengün One‑on‑One
articles.original_title: Deandre Ayton Accepted JJ Redick’s Challenge To Guard Alperen Sengun 1-On-1
articles.summary: In Game 6 of the playoffs, Lakers center Deandre Ayton accepted coach JJ Redick’s challenge to match up directly against Rockets forward Alperen Şengün. Ayton’s performance helped the Lakers contain Şengün’s impact, showcasing his growth in high‑pressure moments.
key_points: Ayton embraced a one‑on‑one defensive assignment against Şengün.
His effort contributed to limiting Şengün’s scoring and rebounding in the game.
The matchup highlighted Ayton’s development and importance in the Lakers’ playoff run.

articles.title: Anthony Edwards Could Return for Game 3 or 4 vs. Spurs
articles.original_title: Anthony Edwards reportedly could return to Timberwolves for Game 3 or 4 against Spurs 'at the earliest'
articles.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves are hopeful that forward Anthony Edwards will be cleared to play in the second‑round series against the San Antonio Spurs as early as Game 3, with a possible appearance in Game 4. His return would give the Wolves a boost after missing the first two games due to injury.
key_points: Edwards' injury status is improving, and medical staff expect clearance by Game 3 at the earliest.
His presence would add scoring and defensive versatility for Minnesota.
The Spurs have won the first two games, so Edwards' return could shift the series momentum.

articles.title: Timberwolves vs. Spurs Series Preview: Can Minnesota Slow Wembanyama?
articles.original_title: Minnesota Timberwolves vs San Antonio Spurs series preview, prediction: Can Wolves slow Wemby?
articles.summary: The preview examines how the Timberwolves, who managed to score against Denver without Anthony Edwards, will fare against a very different San Antonio team led by Victor Wembanyama. Analysts question whether Minnesota’s offensive adjustments will be enough to contain the Spurs’ star and keep the series competitive.
key_points: Minnesota showed resilience in the first round despite missing Edwards.
San Antonio relies heavily on Victor Wembanyama’s interior dominance.
Key matchup focus: Wolves’ perimeter shooting versus Spurs’ defense anchored by Wembanyama.

articles.title: 76ers Limit Ticket Access to Block Knicks Fans in Second‑Round Series
articles.original_title: 76ers restrict ticket access to fans in Philadelphia to keep out Knicks fans in second-round playoff series
articles.summary: Philadelphia is restricting ticket sales for the upcoming playoff games to prevent a large influx of New York Knicks supporters from traveling to the Spectrum. The move aims to preserve home‑court advantage for the Sixers as they host the Knicks in the Eastern Conference semifinals.
key_points: Ticket purchases are limited to Philadelphia residents and verified season ticket holders.
The Sixers cite security and crowd‑control concerns after previous games with heavy Knicks fan presence.
The restriction underscores the intensity of the Knicks‑Sixers rivalry.

articles.title: 2026 NBA Finals MVP Odds: Brunson Jumps Into Top‑3 as Celtics Exit
articles.original_title: 2026 NBA Finals MVP Odds: Brunson Moves Into Top 3 With Celtics Sent Packing
articles.summary: Betting odds for the 2026 NBA Finals MVP have shifted, with New York Knicks guard Jalen Brunson moving into the top three after the Boston Celtics were eliminated. Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander remains the odds favorite to win the award.
key_points: Brunson’s odds improve significantly following the Celtics’ playoff loss.
Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander is still the clear betting favorite for Finals MVP.
Odds adjustments reflect the changing landscape of potential championship contenders.

articles.title: Knicks Anticipate Large New York Fan Turnout as Series Opens at MSG
articles.original_title: Joel Embiid wants Philadelphia fans at Philly games. The Knicks know that New Yorkers are coming
articles.summary: The New York Knicks expect a massive presence of New Yorkers at Madison Square Garden for the opening game of the Eastern Conference semifinals against the Philadelphia 76ers. Philadelphia’s star center Joel Embiid has urged local fans to fill the arena, while Knicks supporters plan to travel in large numbers.
key_points: Both teams anticipate a packed arena, with celebrities and die‑hard fans in the crowd.
Embiid publicly encourages Philadelphia fans to support the Sixers at home.
Knicks fans are preparing extensive travel plans to ensure a strong New York presence at MSG.

articles.title: Jayson Tatum Misses Game 7 Due to Knee Stiffness
articles.original_title: Jayson Tatum, Celtics decided 'return to play protocol' for left knee stiffness meant missing Game 7 vs. 76ers
articles.summary: Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum was unable to complete the required progression from light activity to full practice within a 48‑hour window, triggering the team's return‑to‑play protocol for his left knee stiffness. As a result, he missed the decisive Game 7 against the Philadelphia 76ers.
key_points: Tatum's left knee remained stiff, preventing the required activity progression.
The Celtics' medical staff invoked the return‑to‑play protocol, ruling him out.
His absence left Boston without its leading scorer for the series‑deciding game.

articles.title: Raptors vs. Cavaliers Game 7 Computer Picks and Player Prop Projections
articles.original_title: Raptors vs Cavaliers Computer Picks: Our Best Player Prop Projections for Game 7
articles.summary: Yahoo Sports released computer‑generated picks for the May 3 Game 7 between Toronto and Cleveland, focusing on player prop projections and betting angles. The models highlighted key matchups and statistical trends that could influence individual performance bets.
key_points: Projected top performers include Pascal Siakam for Toronto and Donovan Mitchell for Cleveland.
Betting angles emphasize pace, rebounding differentials, and three‑point attempts.
The computer model assigns a slight edge to the Cavaliers based on recent efficiency metrics.

articles.title: Brandon Ingram Listed as Doubtful After Walking Boot Injury
articles.original_title: Raptors All-Star Brandon Ingram in walking boot, downgraded to doubtful ahead of Game 7 vs. Cavaliers
articles.summary: Raptors forward Brandon Ingram was placed in a walking boot after sustaining an injury and was downgraded to doubtful for the upcoming Game 7 against the Cavaliers. Toronto had won Game 6 without him, but his availability remains uncertain for the winner‑take‑all matchup.
key_points: Ingram missed the latter part of Game 6, which Toronto still won.
Medical staff placed him in a walking boot and listed him as doubtful for Game 7.
His potential absence could impact Toronto's scoring options against Cleveland.

articles.title: 76ers‑Knicks Rematch Looms as Philadelphia Heads to New York
articles.original_title: A 76ers-Knicks rematch returns Monday, with Embiid pleading to fans: 'Don’t sell your tickets'
articles.summary: After concluding their series with Boston, the Philadelphia 76ers travel to New York for a rematch of the Eastern Conference semifinals against the Knicks, who won the 2024 series in six games. Joel Embiid urged fans not to sell their tickets, while Jalen Brunson and Tyrese Maxey delivered notable performances in the previous round.
key_points: The series revisits a recent Atlantic Division rivalry that ended with a Knicks victory.
Emb​iid publicly appealed to fans to keep their tickets for the upcoming games.
Brunson and Maxey were highlighted for their contributions in the prior series.

articles.title: JJ Redick Praises Ayton and Hayes for Defensive Effort in Lakers Game 6
articles.original_title: JJ Redick praised Deandre Ayton, Jaxson Hayes for ‘phenomenal’ defense in Game 6
articles.summary: Former player‑turned‑analyst JJ Redick commended Los Angeles Lakers bigs Deandre Ayton and Jaxson Hayes for their "phenomenal" defensive performance in Game 6, which aimed to limit Alperen Şengün's impact. Their contributions helped the Lakers disrupt the opponent's interior game.
key_points: Ayton and Hayes combined for multiple blocks, altered shots, and strong rebounding.
Their defense was a focal point in the Lakers' game plan against Şengün.
Redick highlighted the effort as a key factor in the Lakers' Game 6 success.

articles.title: Lakers vs. Thunder Preview: Can LeBron’s Injured Squad Compete Without Luka?
articles.original_title: Lakers-Thunder preview: Without Luka to start, can LeBron’s banged-up group keep up? - The Athletic - The New York Times
articles.summary: The Athletic preview examines the matchup between the Los Angeles Lakers and the Oklahoma City Thunder, noting that the Thunder will be without star guard Luka Doncic. The article questions whether LeBron James and a roster dealing with injuries can keep pace with Oklahoma City’s depth and youth.
key_points: Luka Doncic is out for the start of the series, impacting the Thunder’s offensive firepower.
LeBron James and the Lakers are navigating multiple injuries, raising concerns about stamina and rotation depth.
The preview highlights the strategic adjustments each team may need to make to stay competitive.

articles.title: Chet Holmgren Highlights LeBron James’ Longevity Ahead of Playoff Round 2
articles.original_title: Thunder Star Chet Holmgren Puts the Longevity of LeBron James into Perspective Ahead of Round 2 - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: In a Yahoo Sports piece, Thunder rookie Chet Holmgren comments on LeBron James’ durability and the example it sets for younger players. Holmgren uses James’ career longevity to frame the upcoming second‑round matchup between Oklahoma City and Los Angeles.
key_points: Holmgren praises James for maintaining elite performance well into his late 30s.
The remarks underscore the contrast between a veteran’s experience and the Thunder’s youthful roster.
The article ties Holmgren’s perspective to the broader narrative of the playoff series.

articles.title: NBA Playoffs Preview: What to Expect in the Thunder‑Lakers Series
articles.original_title: NBA Playoffs: What to expect in Thunder-Lakers series - NBA
articles.summary: NBA.com outlines the key storylines and tactical battles to watch as the Thunder face the Lakers in the postseason. The preview covers match‑up advantages, potential game‑changing players, and the impact of injuries on both squads.
key_points: Analysis of guard versus forward match‑ups, especially Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander vs. LeBron James.
Evaluation of each team’s defensive schemes and how they may exploit the other’s weaknesses.
Consideration of health issues that could influence the series outcome.

articles.title: Luka Doncic Injury Update: Latest Report from Brian Windhorst
articles.original_title: Luka Doncic injury update: Brian Windhorst drops latest news on Lakers star - The Times of India
articles.summary: The Times of India relays Brian Windhorst’s most recent information on Luka Doncic’s injury status, clarifying that the Dallas star is dealing with a minor issue but is expected to return soon. The update provides context for the upcoming games involving the Lakers and the broader playoff picture.
key_points: Windhorst confirms Doncic’s injury is not season‑ending and recovery is progressing.
The report suggests the Dallas Mavericks could be back to full strength before the next round.
Implications for the Lakers are noted, as a healthy Doncic would affect future match‑ups.

articles.title: LeBron James Thanks RJ Barrett for Extra Rest and Golf Time
articles.original_title: LeBron James thanked RJ Barrett for giving him extra rest and time to golf - Silver Screen and Roll
articles.summary: Silver Screen and Roll reports that LeBron James publicly thanked New York Knicks forward RJ Barrett for allowing him additional rest during a recent practice, which gave James the opportunity to play a round of golf. The gesture highlights the camaraderie between players across teams.
key_points: James expressed gratitude to Barrett for stepping aside during a practice drill.
The extra downtime let James enjoy a brief golf outing, a rare leisure moment amid the season.
The story underscores the mutual respect and light‑hearted interactions among NBA players.
